vulnerability.reference: Inter-institutional model sharing without federated privacy safeguards enables reconstruction.
vulnerability.generated: Shared ensemble structures retain fine-grained data signatures, leaving aggregated models unable to prevent exact reconstruction of training records.

impact.reference: Reconstruction of patient-level medical records violates health privacy laws (e.g., HIPAA).
impact.generated: Full exposure of patient medical histories, violating confidentiality, eroding trust in collaborative diagnostics, and enabling discriminatory or harmful use.

dose_response.reference: Shared random forest aggregated statistics leakage enables reconstruction of patient records, which in collaborative hospital diagnosis exposes patients to health privacy violations, institutional distrust, and medical identity theft.
dose_response.generated: Ensemble models leak training data via aggregated statistics, enabling exact reconstruction; through patients contributing to shared ensembles, retained fine-grained signatures make reconstruction unpreventable, leading to full exposure of medical histories, confidentiality violation, trust erosion in collaborative diagnostics, and discriminatory use.
